props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · Nine Weights vs Nineteen Million

beats.narration_text: Hej, Kore. This is Kore, in for Humanitarians AI. Connecting a camera image to just 128 neurons needs nineteen million weights — 76 megabytes — before the network learns anything.

props.command: Why does a convolution need only 9 weights to process an entire image while a fully connected layer needs 19 million?
output: 3×3 filter slides across image — same 9 weights reused at every position

beats.narration_text: Here is why Nine Weights vs Nineteen Million belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

props.command: Why does a convolution need only 9 weights to process an entire image while a fully connected layer needs 19 million?
output: 3×3 filter slides across image — same 9 weights reused at every position

beats.narration_text: The book's comparison says it plainly: nineteen million weights in a fully-connected layer, versus weight sharing in a convolution. Nine parameters, reused everywhere.

A 224-by-224 RGB image has 150,528 inputs. Fully connect those to 128 neurons: 150,528 times 128 equals 19.3 million weights. At four bytes each, that is 76 megabytes before a single gradient is computed.

A convolution slides a 3-by-3 filter across the image. At every position it uses the same nine weights. Spatial structure is preserved because the filter sees local patches. The entire image is processed with nine numbers.
The comparison: 19.3 million parameters versus 9. Six orders of magnitude. That terracotta bar on the right is the one that runs on your microcontroller.

Your turn. Paste this into Claude: I have an image of size H by W by C. Walk me through: one — how many parameters a fully connected layer to 128 neurons needs; two — how many a single 3-by-3 conv filter needs; three — why convolutions preserve spatial structure while fully connected layers don't; and four — what happens to parameter count as I stack conv layers. Fill in real image dimensions from your project and get the exact numbers.

props.command: I have an image of size [H]×[W]×[C]. Walk me through: (1) how many parameters a fully connected layer to 128 neurons needs, (2) how many a single 3×3 conv filter needs, (3) why convolutions preserve spatial structure while FC layers don't, and (4) what happens to parameter count as I stack conv layers.
